note: These assertions are executed via ddev on the live Drupal instance AFTER headless code generation. They verify test files are valid, discoverable, and actually PASS when run. rt-1 MUST run first. SIMPLETEST_DB must be set for Kernel/Functional tests. phpunit.xml must exist (copy from core/phpunit.xml.dist if needed).

assertions.name: Module re-enables cleanly after test files added
assertions.command: ddev drush cr && ddev drush pm:uninstall group_ai_pm -y && ddev drush en group_ai_pm -y
assertions.rationale: Adding test files can introduce autoload conflicts, bad use statements, or namespace collisions that break the module. This confirms the test files don't interfere with module functionality.

assertions.name: Test files exist in correct directory structure (at least 8 total: 6 existing + 2 new)
assertions.command: ddev drush php-eval "$base = DRUPAL_ROOT . '/modules/custom/group_ai_pm/tests/src/'; $kernel = glob($base . 'Kernel/*Test.php'); $functional = glob($base . 'Functional/*Test.php'); $total = count($kernel) + count($functional); echo $total >= 8 ? 'TESTS_FOUND:kernel=' . count($kernel) . ',functional=' . count($functional) . ',total=' . $total : 'INSUFFICIENT_TESTS:total=' . $total;"
assertions.rationale: Verifies tests are placed in the correct Drupal test directory structure (tests/src/Kernel/, tests/src/Functional/). Tests in wrong directories are not discovered by PHPUnit. Threshold of 8 ensures 2+ NEW tests were created beyond the 6 existing ones.

assertions.name: Test files have no PHP syntax errors
assertions.command: ddev exec bash -c 'for f in $(find /var/www/html/web/modules/custom/group_ai_pm/tests/ -name "*Test.php" 2>/dev/null); do php -l "$f" 2>&1; done'
assertions.rationale: Test files generated by Haiku may have syntax errors from incorrect PHP constructs, unclosed braces, or malformed annotations. PHP lint catches these before PHPUnit tries to load them.

assertions.name: PHPUnit discovers test classes with --list-tests
assertions.rationale: PHPUnit discovery validates the full chain: correct directory structure, valid namespace, matching filename, @group annotation, valid base class extension. A test file can exist and parse without errors but still not be discovered if the namespace, directory, or annotation is wrong.

assertions.name: Kernel tests pass (all kernel tests including existing + new)
assertions.command: ddev exec bash -c 'cd /var/www/html/web && SIMPLETEST_DB="sqlite://localhost//var/www/html/web/sites/default/files/.ht.sqlite" SIMPLETEST_BASE_URL="http://localhost" ../vendor/bin/phpunit --group group_ai_pm modules/custom/group_ai_pm/tests/src/Kernel/ 2>&1 | tail -15'
assertions.rationale: Running actual kernel tests is the strongest signal for testing skill quality. Correct installEntitySchema(), complete $modules array, proper parent::setUp() order, and installConfig() directly determine pass vs fatal. Missing any of these — all patterns the testing skill explicitly warns about — causes immediate test failures.

assertions.name: Functional tests pass (all functional tests including existing + new)
assertions.command: ddev exec bash -c 'cd /var/www/html/web && SIMPLETEST_DB="sqlite://localhost//var/www/html/web/sites/default/files/.ht.sqlite" SIMPLETEST_BASE_URL="http://localhost" ../vendor/bin/phpunit --group group_ai_pm modules/custom/group_ai_pm/tests/src/Functional/ 2>&1 | tail -15'
assertions.rationale: Functional tests validate $defaultTheme property (required by BrowserTestBase), drupalCreateUser() with correct permissions, page navigation, and HTML assertions. Missing $defaultTheme alone causes every functional test to fail with a cryptic theme error.

assertions.name: phpcs passes on test files
assertions.rationale: Test files must follow Drupal coding standards. phpcs catches missing doc comments, wrong indentation, and other style violations that the coding-standards skill should prevent.

assertions.name: NEW test files exist for REST API and board page
assertions.command: ddev drush php-eval "$base = DRUPAL_ROOT . '/modules/custom/group_ai_pm/tests/src/'; $kernel_files = glob($base . 'Kernel/*Test.php'); $functional_files = glob($base . 'Functional/*Test.php'); $new_kernel = FALSE; $new_functional = FALSE; $existing_kernel = ['EntityCrudTest.php', 'AccessControlTest.php', 'CronQueueTest.php']; $existing_functional = ['SettingsFormTest.php', 'DashboardTest.php', 'EntityFormIntegrationTest.php']; foreach ($kernel_files as $f) { $name = basename($f); if (!in_array($name, $existing_kernel)) { $new_kernel = TRUE; } } foreach ($functional_files as $f) { $name = basename($f); if (!in_array($name, $existing_functional)) { $new_functional = TRUE; } } echo ($new_kernel && $new_functional) ? 'NEW_TESTS_FOUND:kernel=yes,functional=yes' : 'MISSING_NEW_TESTS:kernel=' . ($new_kernel ? 'yes' : 'no') . ',functional=' . ($new_functional ? 'yes' : 'no');"
assertions.rationale: Confirms Haiku created NEW test files beyond the existing 6, not just kept or modified existing ones. Checks for at least one new file in both Kernel and Functional directories.

assertions.name: Board page still loads after test files added (browser regression)
assertions.command: Navigate to the board page for an existing project and verify the #kanban-app mount point element is present on the page.
assertions.expect: Page loads with 200 status and contains an element with id='kanban-app'
assertions.rationale: Regression check — test file additions should not break the board page rendering. This confirms the module's frontend functionality is intact after code generation.
